title: Exploration of Corporate Landlords and Evictions Data for Boston Housing Research
description: I performed an analysis of a Boston housing ownership trends dataset. The data provided information about landlords and housing ownership across Boston's neighborhoods, and I looked at the corporate ownership rates and owner occupancy rates to draw conclusions about relationships between the data and Boston's housing affordability crisis.
story: As my graduation date nears, finding a place of permanent residence in Boston is becoming a greater concern by the day. The housing affordability situation in Boston is becoming more and more of a crisis, and I want to find out why.

title: Jugglebot: A robotic arm that can automate the task of juggling
description: We made a robot that can repeatedly automate the task of tossing and catching a ball, which is the first step towards creating a robot that can fully automate the juggling process. We used a simulation of the kuka iiwa robot to perform this task, via motion planning and robot control.
story: I really like juggling, but I'm not good at it. I wanted to figure out if I could get something else--specifically, a robot arm--to perform the task for me instead.

title: Personal Home Page
description: I made a personal website for myself using HTML and CSS skills I'd learned in 6.4500 (and also 6.C35!), which includes information about myself, my projects portfolio, my resume, and a form to contact me.
story: Every professional needs a homepage documenting biographical information about their career, and as I enter the professional world, having such a page is important to my career.

title: An MIT Birds-Eye View Game
description: We created a Weblab project that was a game using the MIT Birds-Eye view as a map, and players could navigate around the different buildings and floors to collect tokens.
story: On my way back from winter break, we flew very low over the MIT campus, and I took this pretty picture of the campus, which inspired me to create a birds-eye view game for Weblab (which I participated in that IAP), since it was cool how I could see various buildings in detail despite being in a plane.

title: Pokerbots
description: We used Python to code a bot that can automatically play poker against opponents given a hand, using pre-programmed poker strategies.
story: I love playing poker, and I've learned my fair share of poker strategies, but I wondered if a player that uses my strategies but doesn't have the human emotions accompanying my existence could play better.

title: Virtual Cat Sitter
description: An app where users can have a virtual cat as a pet and take care of it by performing tasks.
story: I love cats and wish I had an actual cat, but I can't afford any now, so having a virtual pet would be very nice.
